questions.questionText: Under the IPC Section 375, the definition of Rape was significantly expanded to include non-peno-vaginal penetration following which amendment?
questions.optionA: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act, 2012
questions.explanation: The 2013 amendment expanded the definition of rape to include insertion of objects or other body parts.

questions.questionText: Which of the following implies sexual intercourse with a woman without her consent or against her will?
questions.explanation: Rape is defined legally primarily by the absence of valid consent.

questions.questionText: The 'Two-Finger Test' (per vaginam examination to assess laxity) in cases of sexual assault has been explicitly:
questions.optionB: Mandated as essential evidence in all rape cases
questions.optionC: Used primarily to determine the psychiatric health of the victim
questions.optionD: Banned by the Supreme Court of India and ICMR guidelines as it is unscientific and violates dignity
questions.explanation: The test has no forensic value for proving rape and is highly traumatizing.

questions.questionText: In an alleged case of rape, the presence of spermatozoa in the high vaginal swab proves:
questions.optionA: That the sexual act was definitively without consent
questions.optionB: That the victim was previously a virgin
questions.optionC: That sexual intercourse (penetration and ejaculation) has occurred recently
questions.optionD: Only that penetration took place, but not ejaculation
questions.explanation: Sperm proves recent intercourse but does not by itself prove a lack of consent (which is a legal determination).

questions.questionText: Sodomy (unnatural sexual offenses) is punishable under which section of the IPC?
questions.explanation: Section 377 covers 'unnatural offenses' (carnal intercourse against the order of nature).

questions.questionText: Tribadism refers to homosexual practices between:
questions.explanation: Tribadism refers to sexual activity between women (lesbianism).

questions.questionText: Bestiality is defined as sexual intercourse by a human being with:
questions.explanation: It is classified as an unnatural sexual offense.

questions.questionText: Which paraphilia involves recurrent, intense sexual arousal from the exposure of one's genitals to an unsuspecting stranger?
questions.explanation: Exhibitionism is a legally punishable sexual deviation.

questions.questionText: The medical examination of an accused in a rape case must be conducted:
questions.optionA: Only with his informed written consent or by a magistrate's order (Sec 53A CrPC)
questions.optionB: Within 1 hour of the alleged offense
questions.optionD: Regardless of his consent, using police force
questions.explanation: Under 53A CrPC, a registered medical practitioner can examine the accused upon police request, requiring formal consent/order.

questions.questionText: Which Act provides specific procedures and stringent punishments for sexual offenses committed against children below 18 years of age?
questions.optionA: POCSO Act, 2012 (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act)
questions.explanation: The POCSO Act is specific, gender-neutral legislation for child sexual abuse.

questions.questionText: In Sexual Offences, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Forensic Medicine texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Forensic Medicine references for Sexual Offences. Subject: Forensic Medicine. Chapter: Sexual Offences.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Sexual Offences scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Forensic Medicine. Chapter: Sexual Offences.
